evals.prompt: Use commit-loom to weave the changes from changes.patch in the go-synth project into a clean commit history. The patch contains mixed feature work (new Reverse function), refactoring (strings.Builder in ToCamelCase), and documentation improvements. Run the skill and produce maintainer-grade commits.
evals.expected_output: A sequence of 2+ maintainer-grade commits covering all hunks, with a ledger file tracking dispositions and a final summary.
expectations: The skill produces at least 2 commits (not a single dump commit)
A ledger file exists in ./scratch/ with valid JSON
Every hunk in the original diff has an explicit disposition in the ledger
Each commit message is non-trivial (more than just 'update files' or 'changes')
The commit messages follow a consistent style (conventional commits or matching repo convention)
The ledger's hunk_registry has entries for every hunk from the original diff
The final summary is delivered to the user with commit SHAs and a hunk accounting
A backup branch was created pointing at the original HEAD
All Tier 1 checks pass (build, test, lint)
Commit sequence respects dependencies (refactor before feature)

evals.prompt: Use commit-loom to weave the changes from changes.patch in the ts-synth project into a clean commit history. The patch contains new timing utilities (debounce/throttle/sleep), a validators refactor, and string-utils additions. Run the skill and produce maintainer-grade commits.
evals.expected_output: A sequence of 2+ maintainer-grade commits covering all hunks, with a ledger file tracking dispositions and a final summary.
expectations: The skill produces at least 2 commits (not a single dump commit)
A ledger file exists in ./scratch/ with valid JSON
Every hunk in the original diff has an explicit disposition in the ledger
Each commit message is non-trivial (more than just 'update files' or 'changes')
The commit messages follow a consistent style (conventional commits or matching repo convention)
The ledger's hunk_registry has entries for every hunk from the original diff
The final summary is delivered to the user with commit SHAs and a hunk accounting
A backup branch was created pointing at the original HEAD
All Tier 1 checks pass (build, test, lint)
Commit sequence respects dependencies (foundational utilities before timing features)
